outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Fifth US-Iran nuclear talks in Rome end without breakthrough; enrichment remains core divide
outlets.summary: The fifth round of indirect nuclear negotiations in Rome concluded May 23 with "some but not conclusive progress." Iran's Araghchi publicly stated that zero enrichment means no deal, while Mojtaba Khamenei barred any export of enriched uranium stockpiles.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Iran rebuilding military faster than expected, already producing drones — US intel
outlets.summary: US intelligence agencies say Iran has exceeded all reconstitution timelines, restarting drone production and regaining 90% of underground missile facilities during the ceasefire — a primary driver behind Trump's consideration of resumed strikes.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Republicans call off vote on Iran war resolution that was on the verge of passing
outlets.summary: GOP House leaders pulled a War Powers Resolution vote after it became clear bipartisan support would have passed it. Democrats called it 'cowardly.' The vote was delayed until June after Memorial Day recess.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump convenes NSC war council on Iran; weighs decisive strike if Rome talks collapse
outlets.summary: Trump convened VP Vance, Defense Secretary Hegseth, CIA Director Ratcliffe, and CoS Wiles for a Saturday war council on Iran. Source: Trump discussed a "decisive" major military operation to end the conflict and declare victory if talks fail.

outlets.name: NY Post
outlets.headline: Rubio warns NATO allies: Plan B for Hormuz means 'collective military mission'
outlets.summary: At the Sweden NATO meeting, Rubio told allies they must prepare a military Plan B for forcing Hormuz open if Iran refuses. NATO's Rutte called the blockade a 'direct assault on freedom of navigation.' Assets being pre-positioned.

outlets.name: The Hill
outlets.headline: Bolton calls Iran talks 'waste of oxygen,' urges Trump to end ceasefire immediately
outlets.summary: Former NSA Bolton called Iran ceasefire talks 'a waste of oxygen' and urged Trump to either end the ceasefire entirely or reopen Hormuz by military force, arguing the pause has only benefited Iran by allowing military reconstitution.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Tehran says diplomacy continues but no deal yet as fifth Rome talks end without breakthrough
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera's May 23 liveblog tracks ongoing diplomatic activity: fifth Rome talks ended without a deal, Iran's Araghchi insists enrichment is a red line, Mojtaba Khamenei banned HEU export, and Hezbollah wounded 8 IDF soldiers in northern Israel.

outlets.name: PBS NewsHour
outlets.headline: Iran and US hold fifth round of nuclear talks in Rome, enrichment remains key issue
outlets.summary: Fifth round of US-Iran talks mediated by Oman in Rome ended with Busaidi describing "some but not conclusive progress." Witkoff said both sides agreed to meet again. Enrichment remains the core unresolved dispute.

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Iran Rejects US Uranium Demand as Pakistan Renews Mediation Push
outlets.summary: Iran's Mojtaba Khamenei directive that enriched uranium must stay in Iran directly blocks Trump's central condition. Bloomberg notes the gap on HEU and Hormuz tolls may be unbridgeable in the current framework.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Trump moving toward ordering Iran strikes but no final decision; NSC meets Saturday
outlets.summary: Times of Israel liveblog tracks Trump holding NSC war council May 23, considering "decisive" operation against Iran. Hezbollah wounds 8 IDF soldiers at northern Israel base including Smotrich's son. Fifth Rome talks end without breakthrough.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Trump cancels golf trip, holds war council as Iran deal hangs in balance
outlets.summary: Trump returned to the White House for NSC meetings as Al-Arabiya leaked an 8-point ceasefire draft. Haaretz notes Pakistan's Munir in Tehran and Qatar's new mediation role as both sides probe for a deal that still excludes nuclear issues.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Netanyahu urges Trump to 'finish the job' on Iran as deal deadline looms
outlets.summary: Israeli PM Netanyahu and Defence Minister Katz are eager to resume war on Iran — Katz said Israel is ready to bomb Iran 'back to the Stone Ages' upon US green light. But analysts say Israel cannot act unilaterally without US permission.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: IRGC: Iran has regained 90% of missile facilities; drone production restarted; US threats will not intimidate
outlets.summary: Iran's IRGC intelligence publicly acknowledged that Iran's military reconstitution has exceeded US timelines, with drone production restarted and 90% of missile launch facilities operational. IRGC warned that renewed US aggression will spread the war "far beyond the region."

outlets.name: IRNA / PressTV
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian commits full government support to strengthen Iran's armed forces
outlets.summary: President Pezeshkian pledged 'all available capacities' to modernizing the Iranian Armed Forces in a meeting with the Army Commander-in-Chief, praising the military's 'decisive, intelligent, and deterrent' responses during the conflict.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Iran pursuing talks 'in good faith' but views US with 'deep suspicion' — Baghaei
outlets.summary: Iran's Foreign Ministry said it is engaging in talks in good faith but warned the US of 'deep suspicion' over American intentions. Iran retained its enriched uranium inside country per Mojtaba Khamenei directive — directly blocking Trump's core demand.
